pattern: Because duplication makes the result grow exponentially, you record only the length after each step and then unwind the operations to map the queried index back to an original letter.
hints: Never materialize the string; the duplications can make it astronomically long. Track its length after each operation instead.
Replay the operations forward to get the length timeline, then walk that timeline backward, rewriting the target index as each operation is undone.

Undoing a duplicate folds the index into the first half by modulo the prior length; undoing a reverse flips the index against the length; undoing a removal leaves it; a letter resolves it when the index points at the just-added position.

edge_inputs.description: in-range index after reverse and pop
duplicate then pop then duplicate, mid index
index out of bounds yields the dot sentinel
index zero of a single letter
reverse changes which letter sits at index 0
duplication maps a high index back into the first copy
out of bounds on an empty result

stress.description: moderate strings with a random in-or-out-of-range k against the capped length
stress.note: k can land out of bounds to exercise the dot sentinel
